scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: Bird extracted 7 rules with confidence reasoning and surfaced extensive clarifying questions via escalations (4 major ambiguities with options). Demand is clearly identified as undefined. Confidence level is 30, well within the <=40 threshold. However, Bird wrote 6 full Given/When/Then acceptance criteria (AC-1 through AC-6), which the rubric explicitly requires should NOT be present for a pass. The rules lack individual confidence ratings (low/medium/high per rule). The domain analysis is also quite comprehensive for such vague input, approaching a 'complete domain analysis as if input were fully specified' failure mode.

observations.text: 7 business rules extracted, exceeding the 5-rule minimum
Confidence level of 30 is appropriately low (<=40 threshold met)
Demand explicitly called out as UNDEFINED in ubiquitous language and escalations
4 detailed escalations covering demand definition, real-time meaning, guardrails, and competitive vs profitable tension
No specific values invented and presented as requirements — thresholds are left as 'configured'
6 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria were written (AC-1 through AC-6), directly violating the 'No Given/When/Then criteria written' pass requirement
Business rules lack individual confidence ratings (low/medium/high per rule) — only invariant flags are provided
Domain analysis is very thorough (business impact, 7 stakeholders, 7 edge cases) which approaches treating the input as fully specified
Clarifying questions are embedded in escalations rather than surfaced as a distinct list — while substantive, they cover 4 topics rather than the 5+ required for pass

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"CEO has described a new dynamic pricing product called DynaPricing at an all-hands meeting.
